!self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the nodes in this group.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n        &self.nodes\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.nodes.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Topology {\n    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n\n    /// Replica groups.\n    groups: Vec<Group>,\n\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n    rf: usize,\n}\n\nimpl Topology {\n    /// Create a new empty topology.\n    pub fn new(rf: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n            groups: Vec::new(),\n            rf,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to the topology.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n\n        // Ensure group exists\n        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n        }\n\n        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n    }\n\n    /// Get a node by ID.\n    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n        self.nodes.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all nodes.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n        self.nodes.values()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a group by ID.\n    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over all groups.\n    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n        self.groups.iter()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the replication factor.\n    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n        self.rf\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.groups.len() as u32\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        );\n\n        // Joining status is not healthy\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Healthy status is healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Draining status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Failed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Removed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_node_count() {\n        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n\n        // Adding duplicate node doesn't increase count\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_replica_group_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(2);\n\n        // Empty topology has 0 groups\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 0);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 0\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 1);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 1\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n\n        // Add more nodes to existing groups\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node3\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n State Transition Rules\\n    ///\\n    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\\n    /// |------|-----|-------------|\\n    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\\n    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\\n    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\\n    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\\n    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\\n    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\\n    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\\n    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\\n    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\\n        match (self, new_status) {\\n            // Initial state\\n            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\\n\\n            // Normal operations\\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\\n\\n            // Failure and recovery\\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\\n\\n            // Degradation\\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\\n            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\\n\\n            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\\n            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\\n\\n            // Same state is always valid\\n            (s, t) if s == t => true,\\n\\n            // All other transitions are invalid\\n            _ => false,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\\n    ///\\n    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\\n    ///\\n    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\\n    ///\\n    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\\n    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\\n    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\\n    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\\n    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\\n    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\\n    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\\n    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\\n    ///\\n    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\\n    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard is one of the shards being migrated\\n    /// off this node. In that case, the node is NOT eligible for writes to that shard.\\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\\n        match self {\\n            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\\n            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\\n            NodeStatus::Draining => draining_shard.is_none(),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\nimpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n        match self {\\n            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \\\"healthy\\\"),\\n            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \\\"degraded\\\"),\\n            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \\\"active\\\"),\\n            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \\\"joining\\\"),\\n            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \\\"draining\\\"),\\n            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \\\"failed\\\"),\\n            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \\\"removed\\\"),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\"}"}},"session_id":"1ed8f7d8-77b7-4b95-b61c-3826db9496e2","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"752eb94d-3fe2-4e91-87cc-460f864bfde0"}

State Transition Rules\n    ///\n    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n        match (self, new_status) {\n            // Initial state\n            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Normal operations\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n\n            // Failure and recovery\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Degradation\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n\n            // Same state is always valid\n            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n\n            // All other transitions are invalid\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n    ///\n    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n    ///\n    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n    ///\n    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n    ///\n    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard is one of the shards being migrated\n    /// off this node. In that case, the node is NOT eligible for writes to that shard.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n            NodeStatus::Draining => draining_shard.is_none(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n        }\n    }\n}"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"1ed8f7d8-77b7-4b95-b61c-3826db9496e2","uuid":"01670859-734b-47d4-b572-1b37bf2e1b05"}

Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Unique identifier for a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeId(String);\n\nimpl NodeId {\n    /// Create a new NodeId.\n    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n        Self(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for NodeId {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        Self(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\n/// Health status of a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum NodeStatus {\n    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n    Healthy,\n    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n    Degraded,\n    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n    Active,\n    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n    Joining,\n    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n    Draining,\n    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n    Failed,\n    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n    Removed,\n}\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Node {\n    /// Unique node identifier.\n    pub id: NodeId,\n\n    /// Node base URL.\n    pub url: String,\n\n    /// Current health status.\n    pub status: NodeStatus,\n\n    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Node {\n    /// Create a new node.\n    pub fn new(id: NodeId, url: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            url,\n            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n    }\n}\n\n/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n///\n/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Group {\n    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n    pub id: u32,\n\n    /// Nodes in this group.\n    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n}\n\nimpl Group {\n    /// Create a new group.\n    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to this group.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the nodes in this group.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n        &self.nodes\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.nodes.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Topology {\n    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n\n    /// Replica groups.\n    groups: Vec<Group>,\n\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n    rf: usize,\n}\n\nimpl Topology {\n    /// Create a new empty topology.\n    pub fn new(rf: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n            groups: Vec::new(),\n            rf,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to the topology.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n\n        // Ensure group exists\n        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n        }\n\n        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n    }\n\n    /// Get a node by ID.\n    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n        self.nodes.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all nodes.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n        self.nodes.values()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a group by ID.\n    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over all groups.\n    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n        self.groups.iter()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the replication factor.\n    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n        self.rf\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.groups.len() as u32\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        );\n\n        // Joining status is not healthy\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Healthy status is healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Draining status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Failed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Removed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_node_count() {\n        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n\n        // Adding duplicate node doesn't increase count\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn nodes to group 0\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 1);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 1\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n\n        // Add more nodes to existing groups\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node3\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_nodes_iter() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n assert_eq!(nodes.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_groups_iter() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":51,"oldLines":6,"newStart":51,"newLines":93,"lines":["     Removed,"," }"," ","+impl NodeStatus {","+    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.","+    ///","+    /// # State Transition Rules","+    ///","+    /// | From | To | Triggered by |","+    /// |------|-----|-------------|","+    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |","+    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |","+    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |","+    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |","+    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |","+    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |","+    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |","+    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |","+    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {","+        match (self, new_status) {","+            // Initial state","+            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,","+","+            // Normal operations","+            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,","+            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,","+","+            // Failure and recovery","+            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,","+            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,","+            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,","+","+            // Degradation","+            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,","+            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,","+            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,","+","+            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)","+            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,","+            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,","+","+            // Same state is always valid","+            (s, t) if s == t => true,","+","+            // All other transitions are invalid","+            _ => false,","+        }","+    }","+","+    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.","+    ///","+    /// # Write Eligibility Rules","+    ///","+    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:","+    ///","+    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |","+    /// |--------|----------------|-------|","+    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |","+    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |","+    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |","+    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |","+    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |","+    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |","+    ///","+    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node","+    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard is one of the shards being migrated","+    /// off this node. In that case, the node is NOT eligible for writes to that shard.","+    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {","+        match self {","+            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,","+            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,","+            NodeStatus::Draining => draining_shard.is_none(),","+        }","+    }","+}","+","+impl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {","+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {","+        match self {","+            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),","+            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),","+            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),","+            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),","+            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),","+            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),","+            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),","+        }","+    }","+}","+"," /// A single Meilisearch node in the topology."," #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]"," pub struct Node {"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

The caller should pass\n    /// `Some(shard_id)` if the shard is being drained from this node.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_id: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_write_eligible_for(shard_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error returned when an invalid state transition is attempted.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TransitionError {\n    pub from: NodeStatus,\n    pub to: NodeStatus,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for TransitionError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"invalid state transition from {} to {}\",\n            self.from, self.to\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for TransitionError {}"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"1ed8f7d8-77b7-4b95-b61c-3826db9496e2","uuid":"35b50c6f-7661-41bc-ab51-a608e1d0c105"}

Node base URL / address.\n    pub address: String,\n\n    /// Current health status.\n    pub status: NodeStatus,\n\n    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\nimpl NodeStatus::Joining,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new node with a specific status.\n    pub fn with_status(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32, status: NodeStatus) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Unique identifier for a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeId(String);\n\nimpl NodeId {\n    /// Create a new NodeId.\n    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n        Self(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for NodeId {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        Self(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\n/// Health status of a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum NodeStatus {\n    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n    Healthy,\n    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n    Degraded,\n    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n    Active,\n    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n    Joining,\n    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n    Draining,\n    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n    Failed,\n    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n    Removed,\n}\n\nimpl NodeStatus {\n    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.\n    ///\n    /// # State Transition Rules\n    ///\n    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n        match (self, new_status) {\n            // Initial state\n            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Normal operations\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n\n            // Failure and recovery\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Degradation\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n\n            // Same state is always valid\n            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n\n            // All other transitions are invalid\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n    ///\n    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n    ///\n    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n    ///\n    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n    ///\n    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard is one of the shards being migrated\n    /// off this node. In that case, the node is NOT eligible for writes to that shard.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n            NodeStatus::Draining => draining_shard.is_none(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Node {\n    /// Unique node identifier.\n    pub id: NodeId,\n\n    /// Node base URL.\n    pub url: String,\n\n    /// Current health status.\n    pub status: NodeStatus,\n\n    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Node {\n    /// Create a new node.\n    pub fn new(id: NodeId, url: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            url,\n            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n    }\n}\n\n/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n///\n/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Group {\n    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n    pub id: u32,\n\n    /// Nodes in this group.\n    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n}\n\nimpl Group {\n    /// Create a new group.\n    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to this group.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the nodes in this group.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n        &self.nodes\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.nodes.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Topology {\n    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n\n    /// Replica groups.\n    groups: Vec<Group>,\n\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n    rf: usize,\n}\n\nimpl Topology {\n    /// Create a new empty topology.\n    pub fn new(rf: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n            groups: Vec::new(),\n            rf,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to the topology.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n\n        // Ensure group exists\n        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n        }\n\n        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n    }\n\n    /// Get a node by ID.\n    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n        self.nodes.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all nodes.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n        self.nodes.values()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a group by ID.\n    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over all groups.\n    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n        self.groups.iter()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the replication factor.\n    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n        self.rf\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.groups.len() as u32\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        );\n\n        // Joining status is not healthy\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Healthy status is healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Draining status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Failed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Removed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_node_count() {\n        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n\n        // Adding duplicate node doesn't increase count\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_replica_group_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(2);\n\n        // Empty topology has 0 groups\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 0);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 0\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 1);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 1\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n\n        // Add more nodes to existing groups\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node3\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_nodes_iter() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n\n        let nodes: Vec<_> = topology.nodes().collect();\n        assert_eq!(nodes.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_groups_iter() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n\n        let groups: Vec<_> = topology.groups().collect();\n        assert_eq!(groups.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_id_from_string() {\n        let id: NodeId = \"test-node\".to_string().into();\n        assert_eq!(id.as_str(), \"test-node\");\n Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Unique identifier for a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeId(String);\n\nimpl NodeId {\n    /// Create a new NodeId.\n    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n        Self(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for NodeId {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        Self(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\n/// Health status of a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum NodeStatus {\n    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n    Healthy,\n    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n    Degraded,\n    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n    Active,\n    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n    Joining,\n    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n    Draining,\n    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n    Failed,\n    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n    Removed,\n}\n\nimpl NodeStatus {\n    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.\n    ///\n    /// # State Transition Rules\n    ///\n    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n        match (self, new_status) {\n            // Initial state\n            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Normal operations\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n\n            // Failure and recovery\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Degradation\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n\n            // Same state is always valid\n            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n\n            // All other transitions are invalid\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n    ///\n    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n    ///\n    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n    ///\n    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n    ///\n    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard is one of the shards being migrated\n    /// off this node. In that case, the node is NOT eligible for writes to that shard.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n            NodeStatus::Draining => draining_shard.is_none(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Node {\n    /// Unique node identifier.\n    pub id: NodeId,\n\n    /// Node base URL / address.\n    pub address: String,\n\n    /// Current health status.\n    pub status: NodeStatus,\n\n    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Node {\n    /// Create a new node.\n    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new node with a specific status.\n    pub fn with_status(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32, status: NodeStatus) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n    }\n\n    /// Transition the node to a new status, validating the transition.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(())` if the transition is valid, `Err` otherwise.\n    pub fn set_status(&mut self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> Result<(), TransitionError> {\n        if self.status.can_transition_to(new_status) {\n            self.status = new_status;\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(TransitionError {\n                from: self.status,\n                to: new_status,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is eligible to receive writes for a specific shard.\n    ///\n    /// For nodes in `Draining` status, this depends on whether the shard is\n    /// being actively migrated off this node. The caller should pass\n    /// `Some(shard_id)` if the shard is being drained from this node.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_id: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_write_eligible_for(shard_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error returned when an invalid state transition is attempted.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TransitionError {\n    pub from: NodeStatus,\n    pub to: NodeStatus,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for TransitionError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"invalid state transition from {} to {}\",\n            self.from, self.to\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for TransitionError {}\n\n/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n///\n/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Group {\n    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n    pub id: u32,\n\n    /// Nodes in this group.\n    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n}\n\nimpl Group {\n    /// Create a new group.\n    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to this group.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the nodes in this group.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n        &self.nodes\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.nodes.len()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Topology {\n    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n\n    /// Replica groups.\n    groups: Vec<Group>,\n\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n    rf: usize,\n}\n\nimpl Topology {\n    /// Create a new empty topology.\n    pub fn new(rf: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n            groups: Vec::new(),\n            rf,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to the topology.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n\n        // Ensure group exists\n        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n        }\n\n        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n    }\n\n    /// Get a node by ID.\n    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n        self.nodes.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all nodes.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n        self.nodes.values()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a group by ID.\n    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over all groups.\n    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n        self.groups.iter()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the replication factor.\n    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n        self.rf\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.groups.len() as u32\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        );\n\n        // Joining status is not healthy\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Healthy status is healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Draining status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Failed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Removed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_node_count() {\n        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n\n        // Adding duplicate node doesn't increase count\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_replica_group_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(2);\n\n        // Empty topology has 0 groups\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 0);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 0\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 1);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 1\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n\n        // Add more nodes to existing groups\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node3\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_nodes_iter() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n\n        let nodes: Vec<_> = topology.nodes().collect();\n        assert_eq!(nodes.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_groups_iter() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n\n        let groups: Vec<_> = topology.groups().collect();\n        assert_eq!(groups.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_id_from_string() {\n        let id: NodeId = \"test-node\".to_string().into();\n        assert_eq!(id.as_str(), \"test-node\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_id_as_ref() {\n        let id = NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string());\n        let s: &str = id.as_ref();\n        assert_eq!(s, \"test-node\");\n           .unwrap_or_default()\n    }\n}","originalFile":"//! Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Unique identifier for a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeId(String);\n\nimpl NodeId {\n    /// Create a new NodeId.\n    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n        Self(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for NodeId {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        Self(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\n/// Health status of a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum NodeStatus {\n    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n    Healthy,\n    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n    Degraded,\n    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n    Active,\n    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n    Joining,\n    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n    Draining,\n    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n    Failed,\n    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n    Removed,\n}\n\nimpl NodeStatus {\n    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.\n    ///\n    /// # State Transition Rules\n    ///\n    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n        match (self, new_status) {\n            // Initial state\n            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Normal operations\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n\n            // Failure and recovery\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Degradation\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n\n            // Same state is always valid\n            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n\n            // All other transitions are invalid\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n    ///\n    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n    ///\n    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n    ///\n    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n    ///\n    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard is one of the shards being migrated\n    /// off this node. In that case, the node is NOT eligible for writes to that shard.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n            NodeStatus::Draining => draining_shard.is_none(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Node {\n    /// Unique node identifier.\n    pub id: NodeId,\n\n    /// Node base URL / address.\n    pub address: String,\n\n    /// Current health status.\n    pub status: NodeStatus,\n\n    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Node {\n    /// Create a new node.\n    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new node with a specific status.\n    pub fn with_status(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32, status: NodeStatus) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n    }\n\n    /// Transition the node to a new status, validating the transition.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(())` if the transition is valid, `Err` otherwise.\n    pub fn set_status(&mut self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> Result<(), TransitionError> {\n        if self.status.can_transition_to(new_status) {\n            self.status = new_status;\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(TransitionError {\n                from: self.status,\n                to: new_status,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is eligible to receive writes for a specific shard.\n    ///\n    /// For nodes in `Draining` status, this depends on whether the shard is\n    /// being actively migrated off this node. The caller should pass\n    /// `Some(shard_id)` if the shard is being drained from this node.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_id: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_write_eligible_for(shard_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error returned when an invalid state transition is attempted.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TransitionError {\n    pub from: NodeStatus,\n    pub to: NodeStatus,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for TransitionError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"invalid state transition from {} to {}\",\n            self.from, self.to\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for TransitionError {}\n\n/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n///\n/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Group {\n    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n    pub id: u32,\n\n    /// Nodes in this group.\n    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n}\n\nimpl Group {\n    /// Create a new group.\n    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to this group.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node IDs in this group.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n        &self.nodes\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.nodes.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Get all healthy nodes in this group, looking them up from the topology.\n    ///\n    /// This requires access to the topology's node map to resolve NodeIds to Nodes.\n    pub fn healthy_nodes<'a>(&'a self, all_nodes: &'a HashMap<NodeId, Node>) -> Vec<&'a Node> {\n        self.nodes\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|node_id| all_nodes.get(node_id))\n            .filter(|node| node.is_healthy())\n            .collect()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Topology {\n    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n\n    /// Replica groups.\n    groups: Vec<Group>,\n\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n    rf: usize,\n}\n\nimpl Topology {\n    /// Create a new empty topology.\n    pub fn new(rf: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n            groups: Vec::new(),\n            rf,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to the topology.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n\n        // Ensure group exists\n        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n        }\n\n        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n    }\n\n    /// Get a node by ID.\n    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n        self.nodes.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all nodes.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n        self.nodes.values()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a group by ID.\n    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over all groups.\n    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n        self.groups.iter()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the replication factor.\n    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n        self.rf\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.groups.len() as u32\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        );\n\n        // Joining status is not healthy\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Healthy status is healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Draining status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Failed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Removed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_node_count() {\n        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n\n        // Adding duplicate node doesn't increase count\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_replica_group_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(2);\n\n        // Empty topology has 0 groups\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 0);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 0\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 1);\n\n        // Add nodes to group 1\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n\n        // Add more nodes to existing groups\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node3\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        assert_eq!(topology.replica_group_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_nodes_iter() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n\n        let nodes: Vec<_> = topology.nodes().collect();\n        assert_eq!(nodes.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_groups_iter() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n\n        let groups: Vec<_> = topology.groups().collect();\n );\n        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Joining);\n\n        assert!(node.set_status(NodeStatus::Active).is_ok());\n        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Active);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_set_status_invalid_transition() {\n        let mut node = Node::with_status(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n            NodeStatus::Removed,\n        );\n\n        let result = node.set_status(NodeStatus::Active);\n        assert!(result.is_err());\n        let err = result.unwrap_err();\n assert!(NodeStatus::Active.can_transition_to(NodeStatus::Degraded));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_state_transition_failed_to_degraded() {\n        assert!(NodeStatus::Failed.can_transition_to(NodeStatus::Degraded));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_state_transition_degraded_to_active() {\n assert!(!NodeStatus::Joining.can_transition_to(NodeStatus::Draining));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_state_transition_invalid_joining_to_failed() {\n        // Joining node cannot fail (not yet terminal\n        assert!(!NodeStatus::Removed.can_transition_to(NodeStatus::Active));\n        assert!(!NodeStatus::Removed.can_transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn );\n        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Joining);\n\n        assert!(node.set_status(NodeStatus::Active).is_ok());\n        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Active);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_set_status_invalid_transition() {\n        let mut node = Node::with_status(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n            NodeStatus::Removed,\n        );\n\n        let result = node.set_status(NodeStatus::Active);\n        assert!(result.is_err());\n        let err = result.unwrap_err();\n        assert_eq!(err.from, NodeStatus::Removed);\n        assert_eq!(err.to, NodeStatus::Active);\n        // Status unchanged\n        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Removed);\n    }\n\n    // --- New   // --- Test for node mutation ---\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_node_mut() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n       assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Active.to_string(), \"active\");\n        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Joining.to_string(), \"joining\");\n        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Draining.to_string(), \"draining\");\n        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Failed.to_string(), \"failed\");\n        assert_eq!(NodeStatus::Removed.to_string(), \"removed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_transition_error_display() {\n
