state transition\"));\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"joining\"));\n        assert!(msg.contains(\"draining\"));\n    }\n}","originalFile":"//! Topology management: node registry, groups, and health state.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// Unique identifier for a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeId(String);\n\nimpl NodeId {\n    /// Create a new NodeId.\n    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n        Self(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node ID as a string slice.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<String> for NodeId {\n    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n        Self(s)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\n/// Health status of a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum NodeStatus {\n    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n    Healthy,\n    /// Node is degraded (intermittent failures, still serving traffic).\n    Degraded,\n    /// Node is active and fully operational (synonym for Healthy).\n    Active,\n    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n    Joining,\n    /// Node is draining (graceful shutdown, not accepting new writes).\n    Draining,\n    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n    Failed,\n    /// Node has been removed from the cluster (tracked for migration).\n    Removed,\n}\n\nimpl NodeStatus {\n    /// Check if a transition from `self` to `new_status` is valid.\n    ///\n    /// # State Transition Rules\n    ///\n    /// | From | To | Triggered by |\n    /// |------|-----|-------------|\n    /// | (new) | Joining | `POST /_miroir/nodes` |\n    /// | Joining | Active | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active | Draining | `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` |\n    /// | Draining | Removed | Migration complete |\n    /// | Active/Draining | Failed | Health check detects |\n    /// | Failed | Active | Health check recovery |\n    /// | Active/Failed | Degraded | Partial health |\n    /// | Degraded | Active | Health restored |\n    pub fn can_transition_to(self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> bool {\n        match (self, new_status) {\n            // Initial state\n            (NodeStatus::Joining, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Normal operations\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Draining) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Removed) => true,\n\n            // Failure and recovery\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Draining, NodeStatus::Failed) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Degradation\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Failed, NodeStatus::Degraded) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Degraded, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n\n            // Healthy <-> Active are bidirectional (synonyms)\n            (NodeStatus::Healthy, NodeStatus::Active) => true,\n            (NodeStatus::Active, NodeStatus::Healthy) => true,\n\n            // Same state is always valid\n            (s, t) if s == t => true,\n\n            // All other transitions are invalid\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if the node can accept writes for the given shard.\n    ///\n    /// # Write Eligibility Rules\n    ///\n    /// A node is write-eligible for a shard based on its status:\n    ///\n    /// | Status | Write Eligible | Notes |\n    /// |--------|----------------|-------|\n    /// | Healthy/Active | Yes | Normal operation |\n    /// | Degraded | Yes | Partial failures, still accepting writes |\n    /// | Joining | No | Being provisioned, not yet ready |\n    /// | Draining | Conditional | Only for shards it still owns during migration |\n    /// | Failed | No | Unavailable |\n    /// | Removed | No | No longer in cluster |\n    ///\n    /// The `draining_shard` parameter should be `Some(shard_id)` if the node\n    /// is in `Draining` status and the shard is one of the shards being migrated\n    /// off this node. In that case, the node is NOT eligible for writes to that shard.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(self, draining_shard: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n            NodeStatus::Draining => draining_shard.is_none(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for NodeStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            NodeStatus::Healthy => write!(f, \"healthy\"),\n            NodeStatus::Degraded => write!(f, \"degraded\"),\n            NodeStatus::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n            NodeStatus::Joining => write!(f, \"joining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Draining => write!(f, \"draining\"),\n            NodeStatus::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n            NodeStatus::Removed => write!(f, \"removed\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Node {\n    /// Unique node identifier.\n    pub id: NodeId,\n\n    /// Node base URL / address.\n    pub address: String,\n\n    /// Current health status.\n    pub status: NodeStatus,\n\n    /// Replica group assignment (0-based).\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Node {\n    /// Create a new node.\n    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new node with a specific status.\n    pub fn with_status(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32, status: NodeStatus) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            address,\n            status,\n            replica_group,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.status, NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Active)\n    }\n\n    /// Transition the node to a new status, validating the transition.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(())` if the transition is valid, `Err` otherwise.\n    pub fn set_status(&mut self, new_status: NodeStatus) -> Result<(), TransitionError> {\n        if self.status.can_transition_to(new_status) {\n            self.status = new_status;\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(TransitionError {\n                from: self.status,\n                to: new_status,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the node is eligible to receive writes for a specific shard.\n    ///\n    /// For nodes in `Draining` status, this depends on whether the shard is\n    /// being actively migrated off this node. The caller should pass\n    /// `Some(shard_id)` if the shard is being drained from this node.\n    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_id: Option<u32>) -> bool {\n        self.status.is_write_eligible_for(shard_id)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error returned when an invalid state transition is attempted.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TransitionError {\n    pub from: NodeStatus,\n    pub to: NodeStatus,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for TransitionError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"invalid state transition from {} to {}\",\n            self.from, self.to\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for TransitionError {}\n\n/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n///\n/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Group {\n    /// Group identifier (0-based).\n    pub id: u32,\n\n    /// Nodes in this group.\n    nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n}\n\nimpl Group {\n    /// Create a new group.\n    pub fn new(id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to this group.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the node IDs in this group.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n        &self.nodes\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.nodes.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Get all healthy nodes in this group, looking them up from the topology.\n    ///\n    /// This requires access to the topology's node map to resolve NodeIds to Nodes.\n    pub fn healthy_nodes<'a>(&'a self, all_nodes: &'a HashMap<NodeId, Node>) -> Vec<&'a Node> {\n        self.nodes\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|node_id| all_nodes.get(node_id))\n            .filter(|node| node.is_healthy())\n            .collect()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Topology {\n    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n    nodes: HashMap<NodeId, Node>,\n\n    /// Replica groups.\n    groups: Vec<Group>,\n\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n    rf: usize,\n\n    /// Total number of logical shards (S).\n    shards: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Topology {\n    /// Create a new empty topology.\n    pub fn new(shards: u32, rf: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            nodes: HashMap::new(),\n            groups: Vec::new(),\n            rf,\n            shards,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node to the topology.\n    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group as usize;\n\n        // Ensure group exists\n        while self.groups.len() <= group_id {\n            self.groups.push(Group::new(self.groups.len() as u32));\n        }\n\n        self.groups[group_id].add_node(node.id.clone());\n        self.nodes.insert(node.id.clone(), node);\n    }\n\n    /// Get a node by ID.\n    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n        self.nodes.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a mutable reference to a node by ID.\n    pub fn node_mut(&mut self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&mut Node> {\n        self.nodes.get_mut(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all nodes.\n    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n        self.nodes.values()\n    }\n\n    /// Get a group by ID.\n    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over all groups.\n    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n        self.groups.iter()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the replication factor.\n    pub fn rf(&self) -> usize {\n        self.rf\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of shards.\n    pub fn shards(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.shards\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of replica groups.\n    pub fn replica_group_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.groups.len() as u32\n    }\n\n    /// Get healthy nodes in a specific group.\n    pub fn healthy_nodes_in_group(&self, group_id: u32) -> Vec<&Node> {\n        self.group(group_id)\n            .map(|g| g.healthy_nodes(&self.nodes))\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_is_healthy() {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()),\n            \"http://example.com\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        );\n\n        // Joining status is not healthy\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Healthy status is healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Healthy;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Active status is healthy (synonym for Healthy)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        assert!(node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Degraded status is not healthy (intermittent failures)\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Degraded;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Draining status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Draining;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Failed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Failed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n\n        // Removed status is not healthy\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Removed;\n        assert!(!node.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_group_node_count() {\n        let mut group = Group::new(0);\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 0);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 1);\n\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node2\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n\n        // Adding duplicate node doesn't increase count\n        group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node1\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(group.node_count(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_replica_group_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(config.replication_factor as usize);\n\n        for node_config in &config.nodes {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(node_config.id.clone()),\n state.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct ProxyState {\n    /// Miroir configuration.\n    pub config: Arc<MiroirConfig>,\n\n    /// Cluster topology.\n    pub topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n\n    /// HTTP client for node communication.\n    pub client: Arc<NodeClient>,\n\n    /// Query sequence counter for round-robin group selection.\n    pub query_seq: Arc<AtomicU64>,\n\n    /// Master key for client authentication.\n    pub master_key: Arc<String>,\n\n    /// Admin API key.\n    pub admin_key: Arc<String>,\n\n    /// Prometheus metrics.\n    pub metrics: Arc<Metrics>,\n}\n\nimpl ProxyState {\n    /// Create a new proxy state from configuration.\n    pub fn new(config: MiroirConfig) -> Result<Self> {\n        // Build topology from config nodes\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(config.replication_factor as usize);\n\n        for node_config in &config.nodes {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(node_config.id.clone()),\n                node_config.address.clone(),\n                node_config.replica_group,\n            );\n            topology.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        // Validate topology matches config\n        if topology.replica_group_count() != config.replica_groups {\n            return Err(MiroirError::Config(format!(\n                \"Topology has {} groups but config specifies {}\",\n                topology.replica_group_count(),\n                config.replica_groups\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let client = Arc::new(NodeClient::new(\n            config.node_master_key.clone(),\n            &config.server,\n        ));\n\n        // Use master_key from config (already loaded with env var override)\n        let master_key = Arc::new(config.master_key.clone());\n        let admin_key = Arc::new(config.admin.api_key.clone());\n        let metrics = Arc::new(Metrics::new());\n\n        Ok(Self {\n            config: Arc::new(config),\n            topology: Arc::new(RwLock::new(topology)),\n            client,\n            query_seq: Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(0)),\n            master_key,\n            admin_key,\n            metrics,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Increment and get the next query sequence number.\n    pub fn next_query_seq(&self) -> u64 {\n        self.query_seq.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current topology.\n    pub async fn topology(&self) -> Topology {\n        self.topology.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a master key is valid.\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn is_valid_master_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        !self.master_key.is_empty() && self.master_key.as_str() == key\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an admin key is valid.\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn is_valid_admin_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        !self.admin_key.is_empty() && self.admin_key.as_str() == key\n    }\n\n    /// Get node health status for topology endpoint.\n    pub HTTP client for forwarding requests to Meilisearch nodes.\n//!\n//! Implements connection pooling, retries, and orchestrator-side\n//! retry cache for idempotency (plan §4 note on scatter.retry_on_timeout).\n\nuse miroir_core::config::ServerConfig;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse miroir_core::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse reqwest::Client;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Node response with status code and body.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct NodeResponse {\n    pub node_id: NodeId,\n    pub status: u16,\n    pub body: Value,\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub headers: Vec<(String, String)>,\n}\n\n/// HTTP client for scatter-gather requests to Meilisearch nodes.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct NodeClient {\n    /// Reqwest client with connection pooling.\n    client: Client,\n\n    /// Node master key for authentication.\n    node_master_key: Arc<String>,\n\n    /// Orchestrator-side retry cache for idempotency.\n    /// Key: sha256(request_body || target_node || idempotency_key)\n    /// Value: cached terminal response\n    retry_cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, CachedResponse>>>,\n}\n\n/// Cached response for retry deduplication.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct CachedResponse {\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    response: NodeResponse,\n    cached_at: std::time::Instant,\n}\n\nimpl NodeClient {\n    /// Create a new node client with connection pooling.\n    pub fn new(node_master_key: String, server_config: &ServerConfig) -> Self {\n        let client = Client::builder()\n            .pool_max_idle_per_host(32)\n            .pool_idle_timeout(Duration::from_secs(60))\n            .timeout(Duration::from_millis(server_config.request_timeout_ms))\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"failed to create HTTP client\");\n\n        Self {\n            client,\n            node_master_key: Arc::new(node_master_key),\n            retry_cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Send a request to a single node.\n    pub async fn send_to_node(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: Option<&[u8]>,\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Result<NodeResponse> {\n        let node = topology\n            .node(node_id)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id.as_str())))?;\n\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", node.url, path);\n\n        let mut request = match method {\n            \"GET\" => self.client.get(&url),\n            \"POST\" => self.client.post(&url),\n            \"PUT\" => self.client.put(&url),\n            \"PATCH\" => self.client.patch(&url),\n            \"DELETE\" => self.client.delete(&url),\n            _ => {\n                return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                    \"unsupported HTTP method: {method}\",\n                )))\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Add node master key header\n        request = request.header(\n            \"Authorization\",\n            format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key.as_str()),\n        );\n\n        // Add custom headers\n        for (key, value) in headers {\n            request = request.header(key, value);\n        }\n\n        // Add body if present\n        if let Some(body_bytes) = body {\n            request = request.header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\");\n            request = request.body(body_bytes.to_vec());\n        }\n\n        let response = request.send().await.map_err(|e| {\n            MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"request to node {} failed: {e}\", node_id.as_str(),))\n        })?;\n\n        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n\n        // Collect response headers\n        let response_headers: Vec<(String, String)> = response\n            .headers()\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|(name, value)| {\n                value\n                    .to_str()\n                    .ok()\n                    .map(|v| (name.as_str().to_string(), v.to_string()))\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let body_bytes = response\n            .bytes()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"failed to read response body: {e}\")))?;\n\n        let body_json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&body_bytes)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|_| Value::String(String::from_utf8_lossy(&body_bytes).to_string()));\n\n        Ok(NodeResponse {\n            node_id: node_id.clone(),\n            status,\n            body: body_json,\n            headers: response_headers,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Send requests to multiple nodes in parallel.\n    pub async fn send_to_many(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_ids: &[NodeId],\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: Option<&[u8]>,\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Vec<Result<NodeResponse>> {\n        let futures: Vec<_> = node_ids\n            .iter()\n            .map(|node_id| self.send_to_node(topology, node_id, method, path, body, headers))\n            .collect();\n\n        futures::future::join_all(futures).await\n    }\n\n    /// Prune old entries from the retry cache.\n    pub async fn prune_retry_cache(&self, max_age: Duration) {\n        let mut cache = self.retry_cache.write().await;\n        let now = std::time::Instant::now();\n        cache.retain(|_, entry| now.duration_since(entry.cached_at) < max_age);\n
